refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, focusing on communication latency as the critical factor for teleoperation safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency of teleoperation commands and feedback under controlled network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in teleoperation data transmission.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data delivery over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency guarantees.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression algorithms to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to process teleoperation data closer to the vehicle.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams in the NGHE solution.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression algorithms to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Dependency on network link quality and configuration.
Interaction with Safety Services for latency monitoring.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the latency requirement with minimal coupling; edge computing (DP2) would increase coupling complexity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol overhead causing unexpected latency spikes.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms if latency exceeds threshold.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation data processing, communication protocol handling, and latency optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets with latency constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time vehicle status data to the teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from remote operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on latency optimization.
Physical vehicle components have limited direct control over communication latency.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation communication.
Monitors safety but does not directly control latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes latency optimization, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from network link outside Teleoperation control.
Dependency on telemetry data timeliness from Vehicle module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface SLAs with Vehicle and Network Link modules to mitigate latency risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear latency reduction focus and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Latency impact from external network conditions not fully controllable by Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and minimal diffusion, appropriate for DP1 implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across the Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of latency optimization within Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or mitigation strategies for network latency spikes.
Clarify interface SLAs with Vehicle and Network Link modules.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the safety-critical latency requirement with clear ownership and verification approach. No further decomposition needed at this level.

revise.why: Current FR, DP, and IM are consistent and feasible.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data is timely and reliable.
open_risks: Network latency spikes beyond 150 ms p95.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under representative network conditions.
Interface SLA definitions and compliance reports.
